title: NIUM calls on young innovators to reimagine India’s cities
content: The Urban X Challenge Ideathon aims to foster creativity, critical thinking and problem-solving among young minds by inviting ideas that can transform cities into more sustainable, inclusive and technologically advanced urban spaces.

title: Tamil Nadu election results 2026: Full list of winners
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), led by actor Joseph C. Vijay, has emerged as the single largest party in the 2026 Assembly elections.

title: West Bengal election results LIVE: Result on final seat awaited, as BJP crosses majority mark
content: Not only has the BJP been able to hold its strongholds of north Bengal and southwest Bengal but made significant inroads in erstwhile Trinamool bastions of Kolkata and adjoining district.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: CPI suffers major setback, seat count halved
content: The outcome marks a sharp decline from the performance in the Assembly polls of 2021, when the party had won 17 seats.  Despite the overall decline, three of party’s incumbent Ministers managed to emerge victorious

title: ‘Thalapathy’ Vijay: A commander leads the charge
content: C. Joseph Vijay, once known as ‘Illaya Thalapathy’ or young commander among his fans, transitioned a decade ago to Thalapathy (commander), a moniker prescient enough, for he has led his people through one of the most historic debut political appearances in the history of elections in India

title: Nasrapur rape and murder | Child rights body seeks special prosecutor, fast-tracking of the case
content: Sanjay Puranik, Chairperson of the Maharashtra State Commission for Protection of Child Rights, said that the Commission would recommend for maximum punishment for the accused; he also said a special public prosecutor should be appointed and the case heard in a fast-track court

title: BJP’s West Bengal win will strengthen anti-infiltration drive: Assam CM
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma says the Trinamool Congress had been a barrier in checking infiltration along the India-Bangladesh border

title: Tripura BJP celebrates win in Assembly bye-election
content: Chief Minister Dr. Manik Saha, his ministerial colleagues and party leaders participated in a victory procession in Agartala for BJP’s win in the Dharmanagar assembly constituency

title: Assembly elections: Results in four States, U.T. triumph of democracy and Constitution, says Modi
content: The BJP-led NDA has now formed governments in all States located along the banks of the Ganga, says PM while addressing BJP workers at the party headquarters in the national capital

title: BJP’s Bengal sweep fires up Punjab unit; AAP moves to deflate buzz
content: State BJP president says law and order situation in Punjab similar to West Bengal; CM cites crackdown on farmers agitation

title: With defeat in Kerala, Left out of power for first time since 1977
content: Party leaders reject Congress charge of ‘collusion with BJP’, say will conduct forensic review of poll loss to identify and correct ‘aberrations’, both ‘real or imagined’

title: Tamil Nadu election results 2026: DMK’s political journey will continue without any slackness, says Stalin after electoral defeat
content: TVK secures 108 seats, leading over DMK with 59 seats; PM Modi and Congress leader Rahul Gandhi congratulate TVK's poll performance

title: Capitalising on changing Tamil voter mood
content: The leader of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam now occupies a space hollowed out by a politics whose rhetorical power has lost its resonance

title: ​Lost and found: On the 2026 Assembly elections
content: The National Democractic Alliance’s success in Bengal and Assam is partly offset by its failure in Tamil Nadu and Kerala

title: Governance is the ultimate currency in Kerala
content: The LDF’s unprecedented decade long-run has come to an end with the UDF winning the mandate by offering change and speaking to concrete grievances

title: DMK retains core vote share with better seat conversion, but stalwarts lose
content: The party was eclipsed by TVK not only in Chennai — long considered its stronghold — but also in Tiruvallur, Chengalpattu, and Kancheepuram districts. Ministers who lost include Duraimurugan, T.M. Anbarasan, T.R.B. Rajaa, and Ma. Subramanian

title: Assam’s mandate of assertion and polarisation
content: The BJP’s strategy of conflating Hindu and Assamese identities, along with promoting welfare and development schemes, has led to its decisive victory in the State

title: Puducherry election results 2026 highlights: NDA set to return to power
content: The AINRC has improved its tally by two seats in the 2026 Assembly polls, while the BJP’s seats in the Puducherry Assembly have come down by two from its present strength of six

title: The significant erasure of democracy in Bengal
content: The Trinamool government, through its anti-democratic practices and failure to focus on economic development, has lost its connect with the people

title: Watch: Tamil Nadu elections 2026: key winners and losers
content: A look at the key candidates in the Tamil Nadu elections who returned victorious and those who failed to win seats.

title: A new gen campaign and faith of youngsters that paid off for TVK
content: TVK’s campaign was conducted almost fully on social media, spearheaded by young people, who though form only one-fifth of the electorate, says Author R. Kannan; it was almost as if Mr. Vijay got a free pass as other parties were dismissive of him

title: What the winner must learn from the loser in Kerala: No leader is inevitable
content: The LDF’s campaign, like its government, was strikingly at odds with Left tradition and revolved around Pinarayi’s personality; the UDF must now resist the temptation to imitate this model

title: West Bengal election results: Split in Muslim votes spells doom for Trinamool Congress
content: The split of Muslim votes came to the advantage of the BJP and the party’s candidates were seen either winning or leading in six of the 12 seats in Malda, where the electorate is 50% Muslims.

title: Rural sweep and urban gains set the BJP up for a decisive majority in West Bengal | Data
content: With decisive support in reserved constituencies, massive gains in rural areas and a fractured support for the Trinamool in seats with a strong Muslim presence, the BJP flips the script in West Bengal.

title: BJP-led NDA scores hat-trick in Assam
content: NDA bags record 101 of 126 Assembly seats, routing Congress-led Opposition alliance and establishing Himanta Biswa Sarma as one of its most bankable leaders

title: Trinamool ousted as BJP sweeps West Bengal
content: BJP retains strongholds in north and southwest Bengal and makes inroads in Trinamool bastions of Kolkata and adjoining districts; party’s vote share touches 45.8%, the Trinamool’s drops to 40.8%

title: Watch: Kerala elections 2026: key winners and losers
content: A look at the key candidates in the Kerala elections who returned victorious and those who failed to win seats.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: UDF win was made massive by anti-Left voting
content: Senior leaders V.D. Satheesan,  Ramesh Chennithala, and K.C. Venugopal attribute the massive win to cohesive teamwork within the alliance, the guarantees and assurances given to the people, methodical electioneering, and the ‘misgovernance and nepotism’ of the outgoing LDF government.  The scale of the Left’s defeat has been amplified by the loss of face in some of its strongholds such as Taliparamba, Payyannur, and Ambalapuzha, where CPI(M) rebels backed by the UDF posted decisive victories

title: Kerala Election Results 2026 Updates: Voters deliver a decisive victory for the Congress-led UDF in Kerala Assembly election
content: Congress-led United Democratic Front has the last laugh in 2026 Kerala Assembly polls with a historic win as CPI(M)-led LDF falters. BJP secures Nemom, Kazhakuttam and Chathannoor seats

title: In northern districts, the Dravidian majors fight the TVK wave
content: Ministers Duraimurugan, Gandhi, deputy speaker Pitchandi lose; E.V.Velu, MRK Panneerselvam, Premallatha win

title: Watch: BJP takes Bengal, TVK ahead in Tamil Nadu, and UDF back in Kerala | Above the Fold | 04.05.2026
content: BJP wins Bengal, TVK leads Tamil Nadu, UDF returns in Kerala as 2026 Assembly election results reshape the political landscape.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Clean sweep for UDF in Ernakulam as LDF falls flat
content: BJP’s decision to give most seats in the district to its ally Twenty20 backfires, with the corporate-backed outfit failing to make an impact; five segments that backed the Left front in 2021 shift their allegiance to UDF

title: Assam election results updates: BJP-led NDA secures third straight term
content: Congress Assam poll in-charge Bhanwar Jitendra Singh steps down from post; Himanta wins Jalukbari seat

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: UDF sweeps Kozhikode with 12 seats
content: Congress ends two-decade-old draught, wins five seats, K.K. Rema retains Vadakara. All the LDF incumbent MLAs in the district, including LDF convener T.P. Ramakrishnan and Minister for Forest and Wildlife A.K. Saseendran, face defeat

title: Vijay’s confidence and political positioning delivered success
content: At the heart of the unprecedented political churning appears to be a calculated narrative cultivated by Mr. Vijay, who reiterated that the DMK is an “evil force”. But the DMK failed to effectively counter it

title: Kerala Assembly poll: Deciphering the Malabar mandate
content: From an impressive 50 seats in 2021, LDF’s tally has nose-dived to less than a dozen seats this time around in north Kerala

title: Europe, Canada pull together in Yerevan in Trump's shadow
content: "We don't think that we're destined to submit to a more transactional, insular, and brutal world and gatherings such as these point to a better way forward," the Canadian Prime Minister told fellow leaders

title: Kerala Assembly poll: UDF sweeps central Kerala region
content: The LDF drew a blank in Ernakulam, Kottayam and Idukki districts with Thrissur proving to be the only saving grace

title: Political executive will decide on Bengal law and order, says Supreme Court
content: SC was replying to an oral mentioning to continue deployment of Central forces to deter post-poll violence in West Bengal

title: SC seeks Centre’s response on Christian Michel James’ plea in AgustaWestland case
content: He is seeking a release from custody on the ground that he has already undergone the maximum sentence for the offences for which he was extradited

title: Three Indians injured after fire breaks out in UAE’s Fujairah due to drone attack
content: Three Indian nationals sustained injuries after the fire broke out on the Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone, following a drone attack from Iran, local media reported

title: Das Adam Smith Problem: rethinking Smith’s moral and economic worlds
content: The Das Adam Smith Problem shows an apparent dichotomy between sympathy and self-interest in Adam Smith’s works, but most scholars now view it as a misunderstanding, arguing that his philosophy forms a coherent whole uniting ethics and economics

title: 12 Trapped After Central Colombia Mine Explosion
content: Twelve people were trapped after a mine explosion in central Colombia on Monday, the regional governor said...

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Ceasefire On Brink As US, Iran Trade Fire, UAE Reports Attacks
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

content: The man accused of deliberately setting a deadly fire that torched an upmarket swathe of Los Angeles last year harbored a grudge against the wealthy and admired Luigi Mangione, the man accused of shooting dead...

title: Trump Gives "Blow Off" Warning To Iran Amid Fragile Ceasefire, Tehran Responds
content: Araghchi's remarks came as the ceasefire between Iran and the United States was teetering, with two countries trading fire over the vital waterway.

title: Pilot Among 3 Dead, 2 Injured After Small Plane Crashes In Brazil
content: Three people died and two others were injured Monday after a small plane crashed into a residential building in the major...

title: "Assam Made Pawan Khera Eat Pera": Himanta Sarma On BJP's Election Win
content: Himanta Sarma calls BJP's Assam election victory a historic moment and credits PM Modi and Amit Shah.

title: 21 Killed, 61 Injured In A Blast At Fireworks Factory In China's Hunan
content: A blast at a fireworks factory in China's Hunan province has killed 21 and injured 61, Chinese state broadcaster CCTV reported on Tuesday. The blast in the city of

title: Madhya Pradesh Man Kills Wife, Abandons 2-Year-Old Son In Forest
content: Bhopal, May 5 (IANS) A brutal crime took place in the forests of Madhya Pradesh's Raisen and Vidisha districts, where a man murdered his wife and abandoned their two-year-old son.

title: Wanted Criminal Successfully Extradited From UAE: CBI
content: The CBI has successfully secured the extradition of a fugitive and Interpol Red Notice subject Mohammad Navas Kakkat Ismail from the United Arab Emirates (UAE), officials said.

title: From Beaten Protester To People's Voice: Kerala Youth Congress Leader Wins
content: On Monday, A.D. Thomas walks into the Assembly as Alappuzha's new MLA, carrying with him a story that blends grit, memory and a measure of justice.

title: Armed Man Shot By US Secret Service, White House Briefly Shut
content: An armed man was shot by US Secret Service (USSS) agents in downtown Washington on Monday, officials said, prompting a brief lockdown...

title: US Destroys 6 Iranian Small Boats, Shoots Down Missiles And Drones
content: US Admiral Brad Cooper said he "strongly advised" Iranian forces to remain clear of US military assets as it launches the operation.

title: Financial Planning Mistakes The Rich Never Make, And You Should Avoid
content: Most people think wealth creation starts with investing. It doesn't. It starts with control. Wealthy individuals track cash flows closely.

title: BJP '200 Paar' In Bengal, Vijay Delivers Tamil Nadu Blockbuster
content: If the results do not veer from the current trajectory, the outcome would be a churning in the Opposition INDIA block, while for the BJP, it would paint a saffron wave that stretches from the northeast all the way to Gujarat.

title: "Vijay Must Articulate": Karti Chidambaram On TVK Tie-Up, Then A Dig
content: "The vote is for Vijay and he has to really articulate his vision," Karti Chidambaram said when asked about a possible government headed by Vijay,

title: "Hats Off": Amit Shah Lauds Bhabanipur Voters After Mamata Banerjee's Defeat
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Monday lauded the people of Bhabanipur for their electoral verdict, declaring that the outcome reflects the fate awaiting what he described as an anarchic style of governance.

title: 9-Year-Old Girl Mauled To Death By Stray Dogs In Rajasthan
content: Hearing of the tragedy, Lok Sabha Speaker and Kota-Bundi parliamentarian Om Birla visited the aggrieved family at their village later in the day and paid condolence.

title: OpenAI Co-Founder Reveals $30 Billion Stake, Ties To Sam Altman
content: OpenAI's co-founder and president, Greg Brockman, on Monday disclosed deeper financial ties to CEO Sam Altman than previously known as well as a stake in the ChatGPT maker worth almost $30 billion.

title: OPEC+ Hikes Oil Production Quotas Without Mentioning UAE Pull-Out
content: Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other OPEC+ countries increased their oil production quota on Sunday in an expected move aimed at demonstrating continuity at the cartel after the shock withdrawal of the United Arab Emirates.

title: TVK storms Tamil Nadu but falls short of majority: Who will Vijay ally with?
content: Mega movie star "Thalapathy" Vijay's party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), has emerged as a significant force in Tamil Nadu politics, disrupting the DMK and AIADMK's dominance. While TVK secured triple-digit leads in early trends, it fell short of a majority, prompting debate on structural shifts versus celebrity appeal and raising questions about Vijay's solo political path.

title: 'Jai Shree Ram … ': A note, a prayer, and Raghu Sharma's 15-year wait
content: Mumbai Indians' Raghu Sharma celebrated his maiden IPL wicket with an emotional message, marking the end of a grueling 15-year journey.  The leg-spinner, who overcame injuries and career setbacks, dedicated his breakthrough moment to his faith and the franchise that gave him a second chance. This personal triumph overshadowed even a vintage Rohit Sharma performance.

title: Assembly polls decoded: Beyond headlines and numbers - 10 trends behind the verdict
content: Recent state election results reveal a resurgence of anti-incumbency, Hindu vote consolidation, and the enduring appeal of Narendra Modi. Factors like women's safety concerns, the rise of cash transfer schemes, and Gen Z's political awakening also played significant roles. The article delves into these ten decisive elements shaping India's electoral landscape.

title: Microsoft CEO makes a promise to Windows users: We will win back fans
content: Microsoft is doubling down on improving its consumer products, including Windows and Xbox, to boost user engagement. CEO Satya Nadella highlighted efforts to enhance quality, address core user needs, and win back fans.

title: ‘Virat Kohli, call me!’: IShowSpeed’s wild cricket clip goes viral - Watch
content: Internet personality IShowSpeed has gone viral again, this time with a cricket bat, calling out to Virat Kohli in a high-energy video. The clip shows Speed hitting the stumps and expressing his desire for Kohli and the ICC to notice him. This latest stunt further highlights his growing fascination with cricket and the Indian superstar.

title: JP Morgan CEO wants companies to get rid of some managers; here’s why
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon identified bureaucracy as a 'silent killer' that breeds complacency and inefficiency within organizations. He urged companies to eliminate managers who foster such practices, emphasizing the need to focus on outcomes and share information transparently. Dimon advocates for assigning critical tasks to small, focused teams to increase accountability and speed up decision-making.

title: Mumbai Indians IPL 2026 playoff qualification scenarios: Can MI still make it to the top-4?
content: Mumbai Indians kept their slim playoff hopes alive with a thrilling six-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants, powered by Rohit Sharma and Ryan Rickelton's explosive opening stand. Despite the win, MI remains in ninth place, needing a perfect four wins from their remaining matches to have any chance of qualifying. Every match from here is effectively an elimination game. Net Run Rate could come into play, but only if they win all four.

title: 'Get out of your God complex...': What Jensen Huang said to Dario Amodei
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dismisses AI job loss predictions as 'ridiculous,' urging tech leaders to abandon their 'God complex.' He argues AI is a job creator, citing increased demand for software engineers and the Jevons paradox, where efficiency drives greater consumption and thus more jobs. Huang warns that discouraging technical careers could harm the US economy.

title: Tamil Nadu: How Vijay beat Stalin - five factors that spelt doom for DMK this time
content: M.K. Stalin faced a significant political shock with his party's defeat and his personal drubbing in Kolathur. Superstar C. Joseph Vijay's entry, anti-incumbency, federalism debates, underestimation of Vijay, and dynastic politics charges upended DMK's expectations. Despite Stalin's governance record, young voters opted for change, signaling a potential turning point for Tamil Nadu politics.

title: Will Dhoni play? CSK coach's blunt 'above my pay grade' reply goes viral
content: MS Dhoni's absence due to a calf injury continues to be a major talking point for CSK fans, with the star player not travelling for the Delhi Capitals clash. Bowling coach Eric Simons confirmed Dhoni is improving but offered no timeline for his return. The team, currently in a must-win situation, remains confident about their playoff chances.

title: Commanders, Raiders Eye Former Cowboys CB Trevon Diggs in Free Agency Rumors
content: The Washington Commanders and the Las Vegas Raiders are exploring a move for Trevon Diggs as teams reassess needs after the NFL Draft. The former Dallas Cowboys All-Pro remains a free agent following an inconsistent 2025 season. With strong ties to Dan Quinn, Washington could offer the right system for a comeback. Meanwhile, the Raiders need depth in the secondary. Diggs’ ball-hawking ability still stands out despite injuries and recent struggles, making him a low-risk option with upside as training camp approaches

title: Viral Clip: IShowSpeed Lands Punch on Fan During Appearance in Puerto Rico
content: Popular streamer IShowSpeed's recent public appearance in Puerto Rico took a dramatic turn when a playful boxing-style moment with a fan went viral.  During a meet-up, Speed landed a strong punch, causing the fan to stumble, which quickly ignited online discussions and exaggerated claims of a knockout. This incident highlights the unpredictable nature of his content tours.

title: Candace Owens Claims Ben Shapiro Lawsuits; Laura Loomer Fires Back With Criticism
content: Candace Owens and Laura Loomer are in a heated public dispute, with Owens accusing Ben Shapiro of legal attacks and Loomer challenging her claims. The conflict includes allegations about lawsuits, private data access, and media influence. Other figures like Ana Kasparian are also involved. Many claims remain unverified, making the situation complex and still developing.

title: Oil price today: Crude touches $113 per barrel after UAE oil storage facility hit
content: Oil prices surged Tuesday as Iranian-linked attacks and military clashes heightened fears of disruption in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude rose 6% to $114.44 a barrel, while WTI settled 4% higher at $106.42. The Fujairah Oil Industry Zone was struck by a drone, with Emirati officials blaming Iran.

title: Making of Salil Arora: Tennis-ball grind behind 'Bumrah no-look six'
content: Salil Arora’s fearless no-look six off Bumrah was no fluke but the result of relentless preparation. From facing 600–700 tennis-ball deliveries daily to simulating high pace in training, his power-hitting was built with precision. Backed by strong domestic performances and belief from teammates, the youngster’s calculated approach and fearless mindset are now translating into impactful moments on the IPL stage.

title: West Bengal outcome may realign New Delhi-Kolkata-Dhaka ties
content: A BJP victory in Bengal could streamline India-Bangladesh ties, boosting cooperation on water, trade, and security. This alignment offers a chance to revive the stalled Teesta water-sharing deal, a crucial confidence-building measure.

title: Mamatata: Routed Mamata Banerjee fumes at SIRprise
content: Mamata Banerjee's 15-year reign in Bengal has concluded dramatically with a surprising electoral defeat, shattering her hopes for a historic fourth term and undermining a critical pillar of the INDIA alliance. Her relentless drive and progressive welfare initiatives couldn't withstand the dual challenges of anti-incumbency sentiments and rising corruption claims.

title: Rohit-Rickelton script Mumbai Indians' highest IPL chase
content: Rohit Sharma's explosive 84 and Ryan Rickelton's 83 powered Mumbai Indians to a thrilling six-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants. The openers' massive partnership set up a successful chase of 229, marking the highest target successfully chased by MI at Wankhede. Nicholas Pooran's rapid 63 had earlier propelled LSG.

title: Blockbuster debut! Vijay Tamil Nadu's Gen-nayagan
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape is witnessing the rise of a new superstar in actor Vijay, mirroring MGR's trajectory. Despite a contrasting on-screen persona, Vijay's unconventional campaign, leveraging social media and a direct voter appeal, has positioned him as a formidable force. He has emerged from the shadows of established parties, capturing public imagination and potentially reshaping the state's political future.

title: West Bengal elections: Lieutenant to conqueror - the rise and rise of Suvendu
content: Suvendu Adhikari, a former Mamata Banerjee aide, emerged as a key figure in BJP's West Bengal victory, defeating the CM in Nandigram. His political journey, from Congress to TMC and then to BJP, highlights his strategic shifts. Adhikari's aggressive campaign, focusing on corruption and Hindutva, resonated with voters, positioning him as a significant leader in the saffron party's state unit.

title: Kerala assembly election results: Sickle gets hammered, Left homeless
content: Kerala's election results mark a significant shift, leaving India without a Communist government for the first time since 1977. The Left faces a national relevance challenge, struggling to convert its progressive appeal into electoral victories. Recent setbacks in Bihar and West Bengal, coupled with Kerala's defeat, highlight this trend, prompting introspection within the party.

title: Manish Malhotra puts an end to hidden labour with artisan credits on his 'Mumbai' cape
content: Manish Malhotra’s Met Gala 2026 moment hits different because the makers of his iconic cape finally made the headlines, and the designer made sure of it.

title: Assembly election results 2026: Assam & Bengal verdicts shift the minority needle
content: Recent poll results in Bengal and Assam present a political challenge for the Muslim community, amplified by BJP's strategies and measures like delimitation and SIR. These actions, coupled with a Hindu-polarizing campaign, have reduced Muslim influence in certain seats. This trend could encourage similar tactics and push secular parties to re-evaluate their stances, potentially benefiting minority-focused outfits.

title: 3 cheers in Assam, and more: BJP gets majority on its own for the first time
content: In a groundbreaking achievement, the NDA celebrated its third straight victory in Assam, with the BJP obtaining an unprecedented standalone majority. The stunning win, fueled by record voter turnout and new delimitation changes, saw the alliance claim 102 seats.

title: Left out: Kerala gone, Communists not in office in any state after almost 50 years
content: Kerala's voters have ousted the CPM-led LDF, marking the first time since 1977 India has no Communist-governed state. The Congress-led UDF's victory, fueled by anti-incumbency, signals a significant challenge for the Left's national relevance and may impact the INDIA bloc's dynamics.  Defeat was compounded by the win of CPM rebels.

title: Gun scare near White House: Person shot by law enforcement at Washington Monument
content: Law enforcement shot an individual near the Washington Monument on Monday, triggering a brief White House lockdown. Emergency services transported an adult male to the hospital, while a teenager received treatment for minor injuries. The incident, occurring close to a presidential event, adds to recent security concerns in the capital.

title: Assembly elections result: INDIA footprint fades on Bharat map as allies stumble in WB, TN
content: Recent election results have significantly weakened the INDIA bloc, with key allies DMK and TMC facing defeats. This outcome forces the opposition to reconsider its strategy against the BJP's expanding influence. The alliance must now prioritize unity and joint planning, especially in crucial states like Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra, to counter the BJP's narrative and prepare for future electoral battles.

title: Election results: Kerala throws Congress a lifebuoy, but boat still in choppy waters
content: Congress secured a crucial win in Kerala, offering a much-needed morale boost after recent electoral setbacks. Despite this solitary victory, the party faces significant challenges, including a decline in its traditional strongholds and an inability to counter BJP's religious polarization.  The path to regaining national relevance hinges on confronting BJP in its core territories.

title: Assembly election results 2026: Vijay wave, Bengal breakthrough, Kerala reset - 10 takeaways
content: Tamil Nadu witnesses a seismic shift as actor Vijay's party leads, ending decades of Dravidian rule. The BJP achieves a historic win in West Bengal, unseating Mamata Banerjee. Kerala returns to its rotation cycle with the UDF forming the government, while Assam shows pro-incumbency can prevail. These results signal a significant political churn across India.

title: Assembly election results 2026: Top 10 winners and losers
content: The 2026 assembly elections have dramatically reshaped India's political landscape. Vijay's debut in Tamil Nadu, the BJP's gains in West Bengal and Kerala's return to rotation mark significant shifts. Established powers face challenges as new forces emerge, redefining narratives and pushing others to the margins. This analysis highlights the key winners and losers.

title: Puducherry elections result: N Rangasamy’s charisma delivers fifth term
content: all India NR Congress (AINRC) founder and chief minister N Rangasamy won both the seats he contested . he is set to become CM for a record fifth time . his victory in thattanchavady with an increased margin of 4,441 votes unanswered .

title: Bengal triumph promises BJP delayed Rajya Sabha dividend
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Too Maach! BJP angles for more gains in 2027
content: BJP brass is grudgingly recognised by even rivals for never taking its eyes off the ball . the win has reinvigorated BJP's ideological plank, now strengthened by its campaign against infiltrators, and brought into sharp relief the wide traction it enjoys when mixed .

title: Sanghrise in Bengal: No Mamata for TMC as BJP blazes past 2/3rds mark
content: the tribalool Congress fortress fell to a twin onslaught of strong anti-incumbency and a voter roll cut by nearly 12% by the SIR process . a historic win to BJP in a state often seen as the last frontier for a party that's been in office at the centre since 2014 . just a handful of strongholds could stanch the vote haemorrhage as BJP swept large swathes of the state .

title: Kerala elections result: God’s own country red-cards Comrades
content: the magnitude of the victory surprised even the winners . the major factor that contributed to the crushing of the LDF is considered to be public anger directed at what has been described by rivals as Pinarayi Vijayan’s ‘despotic’ tendencies .

title: West Bengal: 'Poriborton hoye gechhe; let's talk change, not revenge,' says PM Modi
content: "this is a declaration of the country's bright future," says a triumphant PM . he hails the party's maiden win in state as a new dawn in Bengal . "we should not talk about revenge but about change, not fear," he says .

title: West Bengal: Amit Shah's 'bye Didi' & 'Anga, Banga, Kalinga' prophecies come true
content: Counting will begin in the morning of may 4 . ballot box will open at 8 am, first round will be over at 9 and second round at 10 . BJP dismantled CM Mamata Banerjee's stronghold for 15 years .

title: NaMoshkar: Bengal debut for BJP sarkar
content: a powerful anti-incumbency wave swept BJP to a historic debut in the state . the saffron surge dismantled tribal strongholds - from border districts to the CM's home turf of Bhabanipur in south Kolkata . "we saw the faces of local bullies," says a schooltee .

title: Assembly elections result: BJP is Bengal Janata’s Party, Vijay Divas in TN, Keralove for Congress
content: BJP finally managed to win the state that had virtually become its final frontier over the past 10 years . the victory of NDA in tiny Puducherry served to drive home the message that smart politics and good alliances can help incumbents entrench themselves .

title: 'Khela hoye geche': 10 moves that helped BJP breach Mamata's Bengal fortress
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: Assam elections result: Masterful mama delivers magic majority
